prompt: Write test cases for the FuelRateLimiter from the design in evals/files/fuel-rate-limiter-design.yaml. Use Go with the standard testing package. Focus especially on the rate-of-change limiting in cruise mode and the stateful behavior.
assertions: Output includes a coverage matrix mapping design elements to test cases
Uses Go table-driven test pattern ([]struct with test cases iterated via range)
Rate-of-change limiting has multiple test scenarios: within limit, exceeding limit, with varying elapsed_time_ms
Stateful behavior tested: multiple sequential calls to verify previous_rate tracking
Every error_handling entry (negative rate, negative elapsed_time, unrecognized mode) has a dedicated test
No test checks only that a function 'does not panic' without asserting output values
Test names describe the scenario being tested using Go subtest naming conventions (t.Run)
Boundary values tested for rate-of-change: elapsed_time_ms = 0, very large elapsed_time_ms
